text: The mucosal surface of the proximal jejunum is visible, showing areas of ulceration.
The surgeon makes an elliptical incision around the gastrointestinal stromal tumor located in the proximal jejunum and proceeds to resect the tumor from the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon closes the resection enterotomy transversely, suturing the edges of the bowel to restore continuity after tumor removal.
The surgeon places stay sutures using three ovigral sutures to secure the tissue in preparation for further surgical steps.
The surgeon uses a 3-0 Stratofix barbed suture to run and close the remaining portions of the enterotomy, ensuring secure closure of the surgical site.
The surgeon ties the spinal sutures, completing the resection of the small bowel.
